item.description: Sarajevo’s status as a post-conflict capital with a growing international conference profile, covering EU accession, reconciliation, investment and Western Balkans regional summits, draws a diverse international delegate audience to the city throughout the year. The FCDO Bosnia and Herzegovina travel advice (2026) advises a high degree of caution, and while Sarajevo’s urban core is not affected by the landmine legacy in the surrounding countryside, the Dayton political tensions, complex entity governance and the need for careful briefing on rural excursion risks mean that professional event security planning is a substantive requirement for all internationally attended gatherings. For the full Sarajevo security context and city-level threat assessment, see our Sarajevo city security overview.

item.title: Event Security in Skopje, North Macedonia
item.description: Skopje’s position as the capital of a NATO member state and an advanced EU accession candidate, combined with its growing IT and energy transition conference profile, makes it an increasingly active venue for internationally attended forums and diplomatic gatherings across the Western Balkans. The FCDO North Macedonia travel advice (2026) reflects standard regional caution, and Skopje’s genuinely low-risk environment means that professional event security measures are proportionate rather than intensive. For the complete Skopje security context and city-level background, see our Skopje city security overview.

item.title: Event Security in Sofia, Bulgaria
item.description:
Event security in Sofia addresses the specific operational requirements of internationally attended conferences and summits in Bulgaria’s medium risk environment. Exercise a high degree of caution in bulgaria (2026); US State Dept Level 2 (Exercise Increased Caution) for Bulgaria (2026).

Effective event security planning for Sofia begins with an intelligence review covering the current local political situation, known protest scheduling, and active Bulgaria advisories. Venue access management at Sofia Hotel Balkan (Sveta Nedelya Square 5) and other principal conference properties requires credential verification, zone segregation, and specific arrival protocols for senior delegates. Secure transport from Sofia Airport (SOF) and medevac pre-arrangement to Vienna or Athens are standard components of the pre-event plan.

item.title: Event Security in Tallinn, Estonia
item.description:
Event security in Tallinn addresses the specific operational requirements of internationally attended conferences and summits in Estonia’s low risk environment. Normal precautions for estonia (2026); US State Dept Level 1 (Exercise Normal Precautions) for Estonia (2026).

Effective event security planning for Tallinn begins with an intelligence review covering the current local political situation, known protest scheduling, and active Estonia advisories. Venue access management at Swissotel Tallinn (Tornimae 3) and other principal conference properties requires credential verification, zone segregation, and specific arrival protocols for senior delegates. Secure transport from Lennart Meri Tallinn Airport (TLL) and medevac pre-arrangement to Helsinki or Stockholm are standard components of the pre-event plan.

item.title: Event Security in Tirana, Albania
item.description: Tirana’s rapid development as an EU accession candidate, Western Balkans investment destination and regional conference hub makes it an increasingly prominent venue for internationally attended forums, diplomatic gatherings and investment summits. The FCDO Albania travel advice (2026) advises a high degree of caution, and while Tirana’s organised crime environment is a background factor rather than a direct operational threat for most events, it elevates the baseline requirement for professional access management, secure transport and close protection for senior attendees. For the full Tirana security environment and city-level risk assessment, see our Tirana city security overview.

item.title: Event Security in Valletta, Malta
item.description:
Event security in Valletta addresses the specific operational requirements of internationally attended conferences and summits in Malta’s low risk environment. Normal precautions for malta (2026); US State Dept Level 1 (Exercise Normal Precautions) for Malta (2026).

Effective event security planning for Valletta begins with an intelligence review covering the current local political situation, known protest scheduling, and active Malta advisories. Venue access management at Corinthia Palace Hotel (Attard) and other principal conference properties requires credential verification, zone segregation, and specific arrival protocols for senior delegates. Secure transport from Malta International Airport (MLA) and medevac pre-arrangement to Rome or London are standard components of the pre-event plan.

item.title: Event Security in Vilnius, Lithuania
item.description:
Event security in Vilnius addresses the specific operational requirements of internationally attended conferences and summits in Lithuania’s low risk environment. Normal precautions for lithuania (2026); US State Dept Level 1 (Exercise Normal Precautions) for Lithuania (2026).

Effective event security planning for Vilnius begins with an intelligence review covering the current local political situation, known protest scheduling, and active Lithuania advisories. Venue access management at Kempinski Hotel Cathedral Square (Universiteto 14) and other principal conference properties requires credential verification, zone segregation, and specific arrival protocols for senior delegates. Secure transport from Vilnius Airport (VNO) and medevac pre-arrangement to Warsaw or Helsinki are standard components of the pre-event plan.

item.title: Event Security in Yerevan, Armenia
item.description: Yerevan’s evolving geopolitical position, as Armenia deepens EU engagement while managing complex relationships with Russia, Iran and Azerbaijan, has made the Armenian capital an increasingly significant venue for diplomatic, energy and technology conferences. The FCDO Armenia travel advice (2026) advises a high degree of caution, and the post-2023 regional environment means that event security planning for internationally attended gatherings must incorporate demonstration monitoring, geopolitical intelligence, secure transport and a tested emergency response protocol. For the complete Yerevan security environment and city-level risk assessment, see our Yerevan city security overview.

item.title: Event Security in Zagreb, Croatia
item.description:
Event security in Zagreb addresses the specific operational requirements of internationally attended conferences and summits in Croatia’s low risk environment. Normal precautions for croatia (2026); US State Dept Level 1 (Exercise Normal Precautions) for Croatia (2026).

Effective event security planning for Zagreb begins with an intelligence review covering the current local political situation, known protest scheduling, and active Croatia advisories. Venue access management at Esplanade Zagreb Hotel (Mihanoviceva 1) and other principal conference properties requires credential verification, zone segregation, and specific arrival protocols for senior delegates. Secure transport from Franjo Tudman International Airport (ZAG) and medevac pre-arrangement to Vienna or Ljubljana are standard components of the pre-event plan.

item.title: Event Security in Antananarivo
item.description: Antananarivo is the base for all international regulatory engagement and event activity related to Madagascar’s mining, development, and trade sectors. The FCDO advises a high degree of caution throughout Madagascar and the 14km Ivato airport transfer is the primary security challenge for international delegate management. Registered security operators, inside-terminal collection, Isoraka district venue access management, and pre-arranged medevac to Reunion are the required event security elements. Source: FCDO Madagascar travel advice (2026); US State Dept Madagascar Level 2 advisory (2026).

item.title: Event Security in Freetown
item.description: Freetown event security is defined by the Lungi airport estuary crossing as the dominant logistical challenge, Private Security Industry Act-licensed operators, Aberdeen peninsula venue management, and pre-positioned medevac to Accra or Abidjan. The high-risk environment – documented armed robbery, post-election unrest, and severely limited medical infrastructure – requires a comprehensive event security plan that goes well beyond standard access management. Source: FCDO Sierra Leone travel advice (2026); US State Dept Sierra Leone Level 2 advisory (2026).

item.title: Event Security in Gaborone
item.description:
Gaborone event security is defined by its diamond sector, SADC governance, and development finance event calendar. Private Security Industry Regulation Act-licensed operators, diamond sector information security, SADC protocol coordination, and the straightforward GBE airport transfer are the key elements. Botswana’s low-risk environment and proximity to South Africa make Gaborone one of the more manageable African capitals for international event security planning. Source: FCDO Botswana travel advice (2026).

For the full Gaborone security context, see our Gaborone city briefing. Delegates requiring personal close protection at Gaborone events should review close protection officers in Gaborone.

item.title: Event Security in Maseru
item.description: Maseru event security combines the medium-risk operating environment of Lesotho – armed robbery risk requiring structured access management at the Avani Hotel and Maseru Bridge transfer – with the specific event calendar of the diamond sector, Lesotho Highlands Water Project, and development finance sectors. LMPS-licensed operators, road delegate management from Johannesburg, and South Africa medevac routing define the event security planning framework. Source: FCDO Lesotho travel advice (2026); US State Dept Lesotho Level 2 advisory (2026).

item.title: Event Security in Mbabane
item.description:
Mbabane and Ezulwini Valley event security is shaped by Eswatini’s agribusiness and manufacturing investment event calendar, the absolute monarchy governance context requiring specific protocol awareness, and road delegate management from Johannesburg as the primary international arrival route. Licensed operators with Eswatini-specific credentials, Ezulwini Valley venue management, and South Africa medevac routing define the event security framework. Source: FCDO Eswatini travel advice (2026).

For the full Mbabane security context, see our Mbabane city briefing. Delegates requiring personal close protection at Mbabane events should review close protection officers in Mbabane.

item.title: Event Security in Monrovia
item.description:
Monrovia event security is defined by the 60km Roberts International Airport transfer as the dominant logistical challenge, LNP-licensed operators with post-conflict operating experience, Mamba Point venue management, and pre-positioned medevac to Accra or Abidjan. The high-risk environment and limited police backstop require event security that is designed for self-contained incident response within the venue perimeter. Source: FCDO Liberia travel advice (2026); US State Dept Liberia Level 2 advisory (2026).

For the full Monrovia security context, see our Monrovia city briefing. Delegates requiring personal close protection at Monrovia events should review close protection officers in Monrovia.

item.title: Event Security in Ouagadougou
item.description: Ouagadougou is not a viable venue for internationally attended events under the current extreme risk conditions. JNIM has specifically targeted international locations in the city, and the FCDO advises against all travel to Burkina Faso. This page serves organisations managing Burkina Faso programme meetings from Abidjan or Accra, and those assessing essential mission options. Source: FCDO Burkina Faso travel advice (2026); US State Dept Burkina Faso Level 4 advisory (2026).

item.title: Event Security in Port Louis
item.description: Port Louis’s event security environment is defined by its role as an African financial centre, with investment fund meetings, limited partner conferences, and SIBA compliance events as the primary international event types. PSIA-licensed operators, information security for sensitive financial discussions, HNWI profile management, and the 50km MRU airport transfer logistics are the key event security planning elements. The low-risk operating environment means physical threat mitigation is proportionately modest. Source: FCDO Mauritius travel advice (2026); PSIA Mauritius.

item.title: Event Security in Victoria, Seychelles
item.description:
Victoria event security is calibrated for the Indian Ocean island context: licensed operators on a small island network, HNWI profile management for financial and private gatherings, SEZ airport transfer coordination, and outer island logistics management for charter and speedboat components. The low-risk operating environment means physical threat mitigation is proportionate, with the emphasis on privacy management and professional service delivery. Source: FCDO Seychelles travel advice (2026).

For the full Victoria security context, see our Victoria, Seychelles city briefing. Delegates requiring personal close protection at Victoria events should review close protection officers in Victoria.

item.title: Event Security in Windhoek
item.description:
Windhoek event security is shaped by Namibia’s extractive sector – diamonds, uranium, and emerging critical minerals – combined with the 42km Hosea Kutako airport transfer as the primary logistics consideration for delegate management. Security Officers’ Act-licensed operators, information security for uranium and diamond sector events, and a low-risk operating environment with reliable South African medevac access define the Windhoek event security profile. Source: FCDO Namibia travel advice (2026).

For the full Windhoek security context, see our Windhoek city briefing. Delegates requiring personal close protection at Windhoek events should review close protection officers in Windhoek.

item.title: Event Security in Asmara
item.description: Asmara is the capital of Eritrea and one of the most controlled and surveilled environments in the world for international visitors. There is no independent media, no independent political parties and no civil society outside government-sanctioned structures. The principal drivers of international visits are the mining sector, anchored by Zijin Mining’s Bisha mine operations, and diplomatic engagement at embassies and government ministries. The FCDO Eritrea travel advice (2026) rates Asmara at high risk and specifically notes the pervasive surveillance environment as a key concern. The US State Department classifies Eritrea at Level 3 (Reconsider Travel), with particular caution advised for the border area within 25 kilometres of Ethiopia.

item.title: Event Security in Bangui
item.description: Bangui is classified at extreme risk by both the FCDO and the US State Department, both of which advise against all travel to the Central African Republic. Active armed groups including the CPC coalition and JNIM-affiliated factions operate in and around the capital, Africa Corps personnel are embedded with national forces, and the PK5 district remains a persistent flashpoint. Medical infrastructure is among the least capable of any capital city globally. The only internationally attended gatherings that should be conducted in Bangui are essential diplomatic meetings, UN coordination sessions and humanitarian operational conferences that cannot be relocated, and these require specialist planning that goes well beyond standard event security. Source: FCDO Central African Republic travel advice (2026); US State Dept CAR Level 4 advisory (2026).

item.title: Event Security in Brazzaville
item.description: Brazzaville is the capital of the Republic of Congo and the commercial and diplomatic hub for a country whose economy is dominated by the oil and gas sector. ENI and TotalEnergies both maintain substantial operations in the Congo Basin and the capital hosts regular investor workshops, bilateral negotiations and environmental compliance conferences throughout the year. The NGO and development community also uses Brazzaville as a regional coordination base for Central African programming. The FCDO Republic of Congo travel advice (2026) rates Brazzaville as a high-risk environment, with political volatility, civil unrest potential and the Pool region conflict context as the primary concerns for event planners.

item.title: Event Security in Bujumbura
item.description: Bujumbura is the commercial capital of Burundi and the hub for the country’s mining sector investment activity, humanitarian operations and development finance programming. Burundi’s significant nickel, cobalt, gold and rare earth reserves attract periodic international mining sector conferences and bilateral negotiations, and the country’s large humanitarian caseload sustains substantial UN and NGO country programmes that generate regular international coordination meetings. The FCDO Burundi travel advice (2026) rates Bujumbura at high risk, with robbery and kidnapping targeting international organisations as the primary documented crime risks. The US State Department classifies Burundi at Level 3 (Reconsider Travel). The CNDD-FDD political control environment, including the Imbonerakure presence, is the defining political context for event planners.

item.title: Event Security in Djibouti City
item.description: Djibouti City occupies a strategic position at the Bab-el-Mandeb strait, a chokepoint through which approximately 12 to 15 per cent of world trade transits, and its five foreign military bases, major port infrastructure and role as the Horn of Africa’s primary logistics hub generate a high-quality international event calendar covering port logistics, maritime security, development finance and military sector gatherings. Houthi attacks on Red Sea shipping since late November 2023 have further elevated Djibouti’s profile as a regional security hub and increased the seniority and risk profile of delegates attending maritime security events in the city. The FCDO Djibouti travel advice (2026) rates the city at medium risk, reflecting a generally stable domestic security environment within a complex regional context.

item.title: Event Security in Hargeisa
item.description: Hargeisa is the capital of Somaliland, a self-declared republic that has maintained a largely stable internal security environment and functioning government institutions since 1991, operating under its own authority independently of the Federal Government of Somalia. The FCDO and US State Department apply their Somalia advisories to Somaliland as a blanket policy, meaning both advise against all travel. In practice, the ground security conditions in Hargeisa are significantly more stable than this classification implies, and a substantial community of international NGOs, development organisations and investment sector participants operates in the city. The key operational reality for event planners is that the advisory classification may affect travel insurance validity, and this must be confirmed before any event logistics are finalised. Source: FCDO Somalia travel advice (2026); US State Dept Somalia Level 4 advisory (2026).

item.title: Event Security in Juba
item.description: Juba is the capital of South Sudan and a significant hub for the country’s oil sector, UN peacekeeping operations and one of the world’s largest humanitarian response programmes. Petronas and Dar Petroleum Operating Company, as well as CNPC, operate in South Sudan’s oil fields. UNMISS maintains its headquarters at UN House, and the 2018 R-ARCSS peace framework generates a regular cycle of diplomatic and technical committee meetings. The FCDO South Sudan travel advice (2026) advises against all but essential travel and highlights armed robbery, carjacking and kidnapping targeting international organisations as the primary concerns for event planners in Juba. The US State Department classifies South Sudan at Level 4 (Do Not Travel).

item.title: Event Security in Khartoum
item.description: Khartoum is classified at extreme risk and is not accessible for in-person international events. The civil war that began on 15 April 2023 between the SAF and the RSF has caused catastrophic destruction across the capital, Khartoum International Airport is not operational for commercial flights, and diplomatic missions and international organisations have relocated operations to Port Sudan. The FCDO advises against all travel to Sudan and the US State Department maintains a Level 4 (Do Not Travel) advisory for the entire country. This page serves organisations maintaining a Sudan portfolio and planning for future re-engagement when conditions permit. Source: FCDO Sudan travel advice (2026); US State Dept Sudan Level 4 advisory (2026).

item.title: Event Security in Libreville
item.description: Libreville is the commercial and administrative capital of Gabon and the principal hub for the country’s hydrocarbons sector, drawing oil executives from TotalEnergies, Perenco and Assala Energy alongside CEMAC and African Union institutional delegates throughout the year. The political landscape shifted significantly with the August 2023 coup that brought the CTRI transitional committee to power, but Libreville has maintained functioning commercial activity and the Quartier Louis and La Sabliere hotel district continues to host the majority of international conference activity. The FCDO Gabon travel advice (2026) rates the city at medium risk, with opportunistic robbery and the transitional political environment as the primary considerations for event planners.

item.title: Event Security in N'Djamena
item.description: N’Djamena is the capital of Chad and a significant hub for Sahel security diplomacy, hydrocarbons sector activity and UN and humanitarian coordination in the Lake Chad Basin. ExxonMobil, Glencore and CNPC operate major oil assets in the Chad Basin, the MNJTF’s regional counter-terrorism headquarters is located in the city, and substantial UNHCR, WFP and ICRC country offices generate a regular programme of coordination meetings. The FCDO Chad travel advice (2026) rates the country at high risk, with Boko Haram and ISWAP activity in the Lake Chad Basin, border-area instability and the transitional political context following the death of President Idriss Deby in April 2021 as the primary concerns for event planners. The US State Department classifies Chad at Level 3 (Reconsider Travel).

item.title: Event Security in Ashgabat
item.description: The capital of turkmenistan and the administrative centre of one of the world’s largest natural gas producers, with a significant diplomatic community and energy sector corporate presence operating under close state surveillance. Event security in Ashgabat addresses the specific risk categories relevant to Turkmenistan – heavy state surveillance, movement restrictions outside Ashgabat, and the regulatory constraints of operating in a highly controlled authoritarian environment – with licensed operators, pre-event venue assessment, and vetted delegate transport.

item.title: Event Security in Asuncion
item.description:
The capital of paraguay and the financial and administrative centre of a regional economy with significant agricultural export, energy (itaipu hydroelectric), and informal trade sectors. Event security in Asuncion addresses the specific risk categories relevant to Paraguay – petty crime, bag-snatching, and express kidnapping risk in the informal economy districts – with licensed operators, pre-event venue assessment, and vetted delegate transport.

The Asuncion event security environment

Asuncion hosts corporate conferences, diplomatic receptions, and industry sector gatherings for the financial services, agricultural exports, and regional corporate headquarters. The FCDO assesses the Paraguay threat environment at moderate risk. Source: FCDO Paraguay travel advice (2026); US State Department Paraguay advisory Level 2 (2026).

item.title: Event Security in Belize City
item.description: Belize City is the largest city in Belize and the centre of the country’s maritime, fisheries, tourism, and offshore financial services sectors. The Radisson Fort George Hotel and Best Western Biltmore Plaza in the Fort George district are the principal venues for international business events, offering a manageable operating environment within a city that carries elevated gang-related crime in its southern districts. Belize’s English-speaking environment and its legal and financial services sector make it an active venue for offshore financial conferences and investment events.

item.title: Event Security in Bishkek
item.description:
The capital of kyrgyzstan and a significant central asian trade and logistics hub, with an active diplomatic community and growing mining and energy sector corporate presence. Event security in Bishkek addresses the specific risk categories relevant to Kyrgyzstan – petty crime, periodic political unrest, and organised criminal networks with documented involvement in cargo theft – with licensed operators, pre-event venue assessment, and vetted delegate transport.

The Bishkek event security environment

Bishkek hosts corporate conferences, diplomatic receptions, and industry sector gatherings for the regional trade, mining, and diplomatic hub. The FCDO assesses the Kyrgyzstan threat environment at moderate risk. Source: FCDO Kyrgyzstan travel advice (2026); US State Department Kyrgyzstan advisory Level 2 (2026).

item.title: Event Security in Chittagong
item.description:
Bangladesh’s second city and principal port, handling the majority of the country’s garment exports and serving as the operational base for international shipping and textile sector delegations. Event security in Chittagong addresses the specific risk categories relevant to Bangladesh – terrorism, political strikes (hartals), and petty crime targeting port and garment-sector visitors – with licensed operators, pre-event venue assessment, and vetted delegate transport.

The Chittagong event security environment

Chittagong hosts corporate conferences, diplomatic receptions, and industry sector gatherings for the Bangladesh’s main port and garment manufacturing hub. The FCDO assesses the Bangladesh threat environment at high risk. Source: FCDO Bangladesh travel advice (2026); US State Department Bangladesh advisory Level 2 (2026).

item.title: Event Security in Dushanbe
item.description:
The capital of tajikistan and the administrative centre of a country with close borders to afghanistan and active engagement from international development finance organisations and energy sector investors. Event security in Dushanbe addresses the specific risk categories relevant to Tajikistan – border proximity risks, political sensitivity, and petty crime in market areas – with licensed operators, pre-event venue assessment, and vetted delegate transport.

The Dushanbe event security environment

Dushanbe hosts corporate conferences, diplomatic receptions, and industry sector gatherings for the development finance, energy sector, and diplomatic missions. The FCDO assesses the Tajikistan threat environment at moderate risk. Source: FCDO Tajikistan travel advice (2026); US State Department Tajikistan advisory Level 2 (2026).

item.title: Event Security in Georgetown
item.description: Georgetown is the capital of Guyana and, since the discovery and development of the Stabroek offshore oil block, has become one of the fastest-growing business event destinations in the Caribbean and South American region. ExxonMobil, Hess, SBM Offshore, and the international finance institutions now bring major conferences and investment forums to the Marriott and Pegasus hotel venues. Georgetown’s event security environment reflects this rapid transformation: significant wealth and international executive visibility in a city that carries elevated robbery, violent crime, and kidnapping risk.

item.title: Event Security in Guatemala City
item.description: Guatemala City is the capital of Guatemala and the largest city in Central America, hosting the region’s principal commodity sector events, regional trade forums, and diplomatic gatherings. Zone 10 (Zona Viva) and Zone 14 provide the primary event venue and hotel cluster for international visitors, concentrated around the Westin Camino Real, Hilton Guatemala City, and the Centro de Convenciones America. Guatemala City’s event security environment reflects one of the region’s highest rates of kidnapping and violent crime targeting perceived high-value individuals, requiring structured security planning for any organised international event.

item.title: Event Security in Guayaquil
item.description: Ecuador’s largest city and principal commercial centre, handling the majority of ecuador’s trade flows including banana and oil exports, with a significant increase in security incidents linked to drug trafficking networks from 2023 onwards. Event security in Guayaquil addresses the specific risk categories relevant to Ecuador – armed robbery, kidnapping for ransom, carjacking, and gang-related violence linked to drug trafficking networks with significant presence in the port and peripheral neighbourhoods – with licensed operators, pre-event venue assessment, and vetted delegate transport.

item.title: Event Security in Kingston
item.description: Kingston is Jamaica’s capital and principal business city, host to the Caribbean’s financial services, energy, and regional trade event calendar. The Jamaica Pegasus, Knutsford Court, and Jamaica Conference Centre in Kingston Harbour are the primary event venues, all concentrated in the New Kingston and downtown areas. Kingston’s corporate event environment is shaped by a significant ambient crime risk: the city has one of the Caribbean’s highest murder rates and documented robbery targeting of international visitors, making structured event security planning a professional requirement, not an optional add-on.

item.title: Event Security in La Paz
item.description: The seat of government of bolivia and one of the highest capital cities in the world at approximately 3,640m above sea level, housing the bolivian government ministries, the diplomatic community, and the mining sector corporate offices. Event security in La Paz addresses the specific risk categories relevant to Bolivia – street crime, express kidnapping, political demonstrations that can block major routes, and altitude-related medical risk at 3,640m above sea level – with licensed operators, pre-event venue assessment, and vetted delegate transport.

item.title: Event Security in Managua
item.description: Managua is the capital of Nicaragua and the operational base for the country’s agricultural commodity sector, government affairs activity, and international development finance presence. The Hyatt Place, Hotel Intercontinental, and Camino Real venues along the Carretera Masaya and in Altamira provide the primary conference facilities for international events. Managua’s event security environment is shaped by two distinct risk categories: the ambient street crime in less managed city areas, and the political surveillance environment stemming from the Ortega government’s restrictions on civil society and international organisations since the 2018 political crisis.

item.title: Event Security in Montevideo
item.description: The capital of uruguay and consistently rated as one of the safest cities in latin america, with a significant financial services sector, regional corporate hub status, and a well-regulated private security industry. Event security in Montevideo addresses the specific risk categories relevant to Uruguay – petty theft in tourist areas and peripheral neighbourhood crime, both at low levels relative to Latin American regional norms – with licensed operators, pre-event venue assessment, and vetted delegate transport.

item.title: Event Security in Paramaribo
item.description: Paramaribo is the capital of Suriname and the staging base for the country’s gold, bauxite, and oil sectors – each of which draws international executives and investment conferences to the city’s principal hotel venues. The Torarica Hotel and Courtyard by Marriott are the primary venues for international corporate events, situated along the Suriname River in the most manageable zone for international visitors. Suriname’s growing offshore oil sector and active gold mining industry mean that Paramaribo’s event calendar is expanding, with an increasingly international delegate profile requiring structured security planning.

item.title: Event Security in Port-au-Prince
item.description: Port-au-Prince is the capital of Haiti and remains the operational centre for the country’s UN, NGO, and diplomatic community despite the extreme security environment. The Multinational Security Support Mission (MSS) has been present since 2024 with a mandate to support the Haitian National Police, but gang control of significant metropolitan areas remains a defining factor in all event security planning. Petionville – the hillside district above the city – is the primary viable location for international organised events, with the Hotel Montana and Villa Creole as the main suitable venues.

item.title: Event Security in San Salvador
item.description: San Salvador is the capital of El Salvador and has undergone a significant security transformation since the Bukele government’s gang suppression programme beginning in 2022. The dramatic reduction in gang activity has repositioned San Salvador from a high-risk to a medium-risk environment for international business visitors, with the Escalon, Zona Rosa, and Santa Elena districts providing a manageable event hosting environment. International investor activity – driven in part by El Salvador’s Bitcoin legal tender status and its active pursuit of foreign direct investment – has increased the demand for structured event security in San Salvador.

item.title: Event Security in Tegucigalpa
item.description:
Tegucigalpa is the capital of Honduras and the base for the country’s government affairs, development finance, and regional business event activity. The principal event venues in Lomas del Guijarro and Palmira – the most internationally oriented business districts – draw regional executives, diplomatic delegations, and development finance visitors to a city that carries one of Central America’s highest crime and kidnapping risk profiles.

The Tegucigalpa event security environment

The FCDO advises against all but essential travel to significant areas of Honduras, including several municipalities adjacent to Tegucigalpa. The Lomas del Guijarro and Palmira districts are rated as more manageable by the FCDO, but the city as a whole carries elevated violent crime, extortion targeting organisations with international funding, and kidnapping risk. The US State Department classifies Honduras at Level 3 (Reconsider Travel). Event security planning must address the gap between the managed hotel venue environment and the broader city risk level. Source: FCDO Honduras travel advice (2026); US State Dept Honduras Level 3 advisory (2026).

item.title: Event Security in Ulaanbaatar
item.description:
The capital and only major urban centre of mongolia, home to mining sector corporate offices, international development organisations, diplomatic missions, and the country’s entire financial services sector. Event security in Ulaanbaatar addresses the specific risk categories relevant to Mongolia – petty crime including bag-snatching and pickpocketing, drink-spiking in entertainment districts, and extreme winter conditions – with licensed operators, pre-event venue assessment, and vetted delegate transport.

The Ulaanbaatar event security environment

Ulaanbaatar hosts corporate conferences, diplomatic receptions, and industry sector gatherings for the mining, resources, and diplomatic capital. The FCDO assesses the Mongolia threat environment at moderate risk. Source: FCDO Mongolia travel advice (2026); US State Department Mongolia advisory Level 1 (2026).

item.title: Event Security in Vientiane
item.description:
The capital of laos and the administrative, diplomatic, and development finance centre of a one-party state with significant chinese and multilateral investment presence. Event security in Vientiane addresses the specific risk categories relevant to Laos – petty theft, road accidents, and the regulatory constraints of operating in a one-party state – with licensed operators, pre-event venue assessment, and vetted delegate transport.

The Vientiane event security environment

Vientiane hosts corporate conferences, diplomatic receptions, and industry sector gatherings for the diplomatic, development finance, and natural resource sector hub. The FCDO assesses the Laos threat environment at low risk. Source: FCDO Laos travel advice (2026); US State Department Laos advisory Level 1 (2026).

item.title: Event Security in Yaounde
item.description: Yaounde is the political capital of Cameroon and the base for the country’s diplomatic community, government affairs activity, and international development finance operations. The Hilton Yaounde and Hotel Mont Febe in the Bastos diplomatic district, and the Palais des Congres, are the primary venues for international events. Cameroon’s oil sector, its position in the Central African region, and the large AfDB and World Bank country operation all generate a regular international conference calendar in Yaounde. The event security environment reflects Cameroon’s high-risk rating, shaped by robbery and kidnapping risk in the city and the broader Anglophone crisis security context.

item.title: Event Security in Caracas
item.description: Caracas is one of the most complex event security environments in Latin America. FCDO advises against all but essential travel to Venezuela, and the combination of kidnapping, colectivo territorial presence, state surveillance (SEBIN), and the logistical complications of Venezuela’s USD cash economy makes event security planning here materially different from other regional locations. Events do take place: the eastern safe zones of Altamira, Las Mercedes, and Chacao retain functional hotel and conference infrastructure. The security architecture required to run them well is substantial.

item.title: Event Security in Douala
item.description: Douala is Cameroon’s economic capital and the country’s largest city, housing the main seaport, the headquarters of most international companies, and the operational base for the energy, agri-business, and infrastructure sectors. It hosts corporate conferences, investor briefings, and operational meetings for multinationals active in the Central African region. Event security in Douala addresses a consistent armed robbery and vehicle crime environment, the political spillover from Cameroon’s Anglophone armed conflict, and after-dark risks that require specific transport protocols for any evening event programme.

item.title: Event Security in Erbil
item.description: Erbil is the capital of the Kurdistan Region of Iraq and the operational hub for the KRI’s substantial oil and gas sector. It hosts BP, TotalEnergies, DNO, Genel Energy, and Gulf Keystone Petroleum, alongside a significant KRG government, diplomatic, and NGO presence. Events in Erbil are substantially more frequent than their security risk context suggests to outside observers: the city functions as a working business hub, not an emergency-only location. The security architecture required for Erbil events reflects a genuinely elevated threat profile, driven by documented rocket and drone attacks by Iran-aligned militia groups, residual ISIL insurgent capability in border areas, and the unpredictable KRG-Baghdad political dynamic.

item.title: Event Security in Jeddah
item.description: Jeddah is Saudi Arabia’s commercial capital and the primary gateway for international business associated with Vision 2030 investment projects, energy sector operations, and the Kingdom’s expanding entertainment and hospitality economy. The city hosts major conferences in the Vision 2030 framework, events associated with the Formula E circuit, and private gatherings of senior Saudi and international business principals. Event security in Jeddah operates against a backdrop of two specific risk factors that have no equivalent in most other major conference cities: the cross-border Houthi missile and drone threat, and Saudi Arabia’s strict legal code governing conduct, photography, and alcohol.

item.title: Event Security in Kabul
item.description: Kabul sits at the extreme end of the event security risk spectrum. FCDO advises against all travel to Afghanistan, and the IS-KP terrorism threat since the August 2021 Taliban takeover has created an environment where mass-casualty attacks at gatherings remain an active and documented threat. The 2021 HKIA bombing, killing approximately 170 people, and subsequent attacks on Kabul mosques, checkpoints, and international-presence locations, illustrate consistent IS-KP capability and intent. This context defines every element of event security planning for Kabul.

item.title: Event Security in Kano
item.description: Kano is northern Nigeria’s largest city and commercial capital, with a significant trading, manufacturing, and agricultural commodity economy. It hosts a range of development sector, UN agency, and corporate events, particularly from organisations operating in Nigeria’s north. Event security in Kano requires the highest level of threat awareness of any regular Nigerian business hub: the FCDO advises against all but essential travel to Kano State due to the terrorism threat from Boko Haram and ISWAP, intercommunal violence has produced documented episodes of city-wide movement restriction, and the general crime environment applies a consistent risk burden on foreign national attendees.

item.title: Event Security in Karachi
item.description: Karachi is Pakistan’s commercial capital and the country’s principal hub for multinational corporate, energy sector, and NGO activity. FCDO notes the risk of terrorism, civil unrest, and criminality across Pakistan and advises against all but essential travel to certain parts of Karachi. The city’s event security environment is shaped by TTP-linked activity, Baloch separatist operations, sectarian violence, kidnap-for-ransom targeting of corporate individuals, and the bandh (general strike) risk that can shut all transport city-wide at short notice. With Sindh-licensed operators, appropriate venue selection, and a bandh contingency protocol, major corporate events are operationally viable in the Clifton, DHA, and Bath Island districts.

item.title: Event Security in Kathmandu
item.description: Kathmandu is Nepal’s capital and a significant destination for development sector conferences, UN-affiliated meetings, trekking and adventure tourism industry events, and multinational corporate gatherings with Nepal or broader South Asia scope. The security environment is elevated rather than critical: the primary planning variables are political bandhs that can ground transport at short notice, the residual seismic risk affecting building stock across Kathmandu Valley since the 2015 earthquake, and petty crime in the main tourist districts. Nepal’s Security Personnel and Private Security Organizations Act 2017 provides a well-structured regulatory framework for the private security sector, and registered Nepali operators provide a capable and compliant security layer for corporate events.

item.title: Event Security in Kuwait City
item.description:
Kuwait City sits at a strategic intersection of Gulf commerce and regional geopolitical tension. Hosting a corporate conference, product launch, or private function in the capital requires security planning that accounts for both the elevated national threat picture and the specific operational requirements of the venue and guest profile.

Threat Context for Event Planners

Kuwait’s security environment is shaped by proximity to Iran-aligned militia networks that have historically targeted US-affiliated installations. Camp Arifjan and Camp Buehring have been referenced in Kataib Hezbollah communications, and disrupted plots in 2020 and 2024 underline that domestic security services remain actively engaged. Events involving US government-linked organisations, American multinationals, or high-profile Western executives warrant explicit threat assessment as part of the pre-event planning phase.

item.title: Event Security in Lahore
item.description: Lahore is Pakistan’s cultural and economic capital, the seat of Punjab province, and one of South Asia’s largest cities. It attracts corporate events from the textile, banking, and infrastructure sectors, as well as diplomatic conferences and investment briefings. Event security in Lahore operates against a formally assessed very-high terrorism threat, a documented kidnapping risk to foreign nationals, and a political environment that can shift rapidly in the period around demonstrations and political confrontations.

item.title: Event Security in Manama
item.description:
Manama is the financial capital of Bahrain and the Gulf Cooperation Council’s primary hub for Islamic finance conferences, investment forums, and sovereign-level diplomatic events. The Bahrain International Exhibition and Convention Centre, the waterfront venues in Bahrain Bay, and the Formula 1 hospitality infrastructure all generate events requiring professional security planning calibrated to the specific Gulf threat environment.

The event security environment in Manama

The FCDO advises normal travel precautions for Bahrain while noting that the threat from terrorism across the wider Middle East and Gulf region remains high. The political environment carries residual sensitivity from the 2011 Arab Spring period, with demonstrations historically occurring in outer townships – Sitra, Bilad Al Qadeem, Sanabis – but not affecting the central business district or primary conference venues. The Diplomatic Area, Seef District, and Bahrain Bay venue zone operate with a considerably lower incident profile than outer areas. Source: FCDO Bahrain travel advice (2026). US State Department Bahrain advisory Level 2 (2026).

item.title: Event Security in Panama City
item.description:
Panama City is the financial and logistics hub of Central America, hosting regional headquarters for major multinationals, international banking summits, and a high volume of cross-border trade events. The city’s USD economy and Canal Zone infrastructure attract a concentrated corporate calendar. Security planning for events here focuses on organised crime exposure, district differentiation, and the transport corridors between hotels and venues.

Threat Context for Event Planners

Panama’s medium risk classification reflects a clear geographic divide. Punta Pacifica, Punta Paitilla, Miraflores, and San Francisco carry a substantially lower day-to-day crime burden than Calidonia or the Casco Viejo area after dark. Organised crime networks with links to Clan del Golfo operate across the country; while high-profile attacks on corporate venues are uncommon, armed robbery and carjacking on principal transport routes represent a credible exposure that the security plan must address.

item.title: Event Security in Phnom Penh
item.description: Phnom Penh is Cambodia’s capital and the hub for the country’s significant NGO, UN agency, and development sector presence alongside a growing private sector event calendar from financial services, telecommunications, and regional companies. Corporate events in Phnom Penh operate in a moderate security environment, with the primary concerns being petty crime and drive-by theft targeting delegates outside the venue, political demonstration disruptions to routes, and the limited local emergency response capability relative to more developed regional hubs.

item.title: Event Security in Port Harcourt
item.description: Port Harcourt is Nigeria’s oil capital and the operational hub for the Niger Delta energy industry. Shell, TotalEnergies, Eni, and NNPCL all maintain significant operational and administrative presence in the city. Events in Port Harcourt are overwhelmingly driven by the oil and gas sector, alongside development finance, construction, and maritime logistics. The security environment is among the most challenging of any major energy hub in Africa: kidnapping rates in Rivers State are among the highest in West Africa, armed robbery and organised crime are pervasive, and waterway access carries piracy and armed attack risk.

item.title: Event Security in Quito
item.description: Quito is Ecuador’s capital and the primary hub for government, international organisations, and corporate activity in the Andean region. It hosts diplomatic missions, UN agency offices, and a growing investment conference circuit linked to Ecuador’s energy, mining, and infrastructure sectors. Event security in Quito has become materially more complex since 2022, when Ecuador’s security environment deteriorated significantly due to the expansion of Colombian cartel-linked criminal organisations into urban areas. Kidnapping, armed robbery, and carjacking have all increased, and the January 2024 security crisis demonstrated that the threat can escalate to a level that disrupts normal urban operations across the city.

item.title: Event Security in San Jose
item.description:
San Jose and its western satellite districts of Escazu and Santa Ana host a growing volume of international corporate events, NGO convenings, and regional business summits. Costa Rica’s democratic stability and developed regulatory framework make it an attractive Central American base for multinationals, but street-level crime, particularly vehicle-targeting and petty theft, requires active management for any event involving principal movement.

Threat Context for Event Planners

Costa Rica is the safest country in Central America, with a democratic track record stretching back to 1948 and no standing army. This removes the layer of political violence and coup risk that affects event planning elsewhere in the region. The working security challenges are crime-focused: vehicle break-ins are extremely common across San Jose, armed robbery occurs in peripheral and certain city-centre districts, and drug trafficking spillover from regional networks creates situational awareness requirements on transport routes after dark.

item.title: Event Security in Santiago
item.description:
Santiago is the most developed corporate event market in South America outside Buenos Aires and Sao Paulo. The city’s mining sector, financial services industry, and regional headquarters concentration generate a consistent international conference and summit calendar. Security planning here is lower intensity than many South American capitals, but protest risk, vehicle targeting, and airport exposure are specific factors that require operational attention.

Threat Context for Event Planners

Chile’s overall risk classification is low to moderate, making Santiago one of the safer South American capitals for international events. The FCDO and US State Department assessments reflect a stable democratic system with functional law enforcement. The specific risks that matter for event security are more targeted than in higher-risk markets.

item.title: Event Security in Santo Domingo
item.description: Santo Domingo is the capital and economic centre of the Dominican Republic, hosting international banks, corporate regional headquarters, UN agencies, and tourism industry conferences. The Dominican Republic is a significant Caribbean business hub and a popular events destination for financial services, energy, and tourism sector conferences. Event security in Santo Domingo addresses a moderate crime environment – principally armed robbery, express kidnapping, and carjacking in peripheral areas – rather than the terrorism or organised armed conflict that defines some Latin American cities.

item.title: Event Security in Tunis
item.description: Tunis hosts a significant volume of international corporate events and diplomatic gatherings, particularly in the Les Berges du Lac business district north of the city, which provides modern conference infrastructure with a lower street-level risk profile than the historic centre. Tunisia’s documented history of high-casualty terrorist attacks, including the 2015 Bardo Museum attack (22 killed) and the Sousse attack (38 killed), means terrorism-aware planning is a non-negotiable element of every Tunis event security operation. The political landscape following President Saied’s 2021-2022 consolidation of power creates an additional layer of institutional unpredictability, and civil unrest driven by economic pressure and IMF austerity has disrupted movement across the city on multiple occasions. For a fuller picture of the Tunis security environment, see our Tunis city security overview.

item.title: Event Security in Yangon
item.description: Yangon operates in a fundamentally altered security environment following the February 2021 military coup, in which the State Administration Council (SAC) seized power and has since maintained active armed conflict against the People’s Defence Force and multiple ethnic armed organisations. FCDO advises against all but essential travel to Myanmar. Foreign nationals face documented risk of arbitrary detention on minimal evidence, with consular access not guaranteed under SAC practice. UK, US, and EU sanctions on SAC-linked entities create compliance exposure that must be addressed in any commercial security arrangement. Organisations that must maintain essential Yangon operations do so under a compound-based security model, with all gatherings structured within secured perimeters and every programme supported by a written emergency extraction plan. For the fuller Yangon risk picture, see our Yangon city security overview.

item.title: Event Security in Bamako
item.description: Bamako’s gold sector and regional finance summits attract a modest but persistent flow of international delegates despite Mali’s critical security environment, which is shaped by active JNIM terrorist operations and the political instability of the post-coup junta governance. The FCDO Mali advisory (2025) reflects one of the most elevated risk ratings in West Africa, and professional event security combining soft-target analysis, DGPN-licensed operators and a tested medevac protocol is the minimum baseline for any internationally attended gathering in the city.

item.title: Event Security in Conakry
item.description: Conakry’s role as the gateway to Guinea’s bauxite and gold sectors draws mining executives, commodity financiers and multilateral representatives to the city throughout the year, but the current CNRD junta context, elevated street crime and very limited medical infrastructure mean that professional event security is an operational requirement for any internationally attended gathering. The FCDO Guinea advisory highlights political instability, armed robbery risk and the inadequacy of local healthcare as the primary concerns for international delegates.

item.title: Event Security in Islamabad
item.description: Islamabad’s position as Pakistan’s federal capital and primary diplomatic hub means the city regularly hosts SAARC-level gatherings, energy investment forums and high-profile bilateral negotiations, each of which carries an elevated security requirement given Pakistan’s current threat environment. The FCDO Pakistan advisory (2025) rates the country at a high-threat terrorism level, placing a particular burden on event organisers to go beyond hotel-standard access control and engage licensed, operationally experienced event-security professionals.

item.title: Event Security in Kinshasa
item.description: Kinshasa’s cobalt, coltan and copper investment profile makes it an important destination for international mining conferences and African Development Bank regional meetings, but the FCDO DRC advisory highlights armed robbery, carjacking and political protest as persistent risks that require professionally managed event security across every aspect of the delegation’s stay. The city’s ANP and DPAS licensing framework provides a regulatory structure for security providers, and the Grand Hotel Kinshasa and Fleuve Congo Hotel both provide a capable starting point for venue-based access control.

item.title: Event Security in Medellin
item.description:
Medellin’s growing profile as a hub for technology conferences, investment forums and fashion trade events draws an increasingly international delegate community to the city, but the FCDO Colombia advisory (2025) maintains an elevated risk rating that requires professionally managed event security across venue access control, airport transfers and delegate movement. The city’s SVSP-licensed security sector provides a competent local resource for event security planning.

For the full Medellin security context, see our Medellin city page. Delegates requiring personal close protection at Medellin events should review bodyguard hire in Medellin for the licensed CPO programme.

item.title: Event Security in Mogadishu
item.description: Mogadishu represents the most demanding event security environment in this portfolio: the FCDO Somalia advisory (2025) advises against all travel, Al-Shabaab retains active attack capability against international conference venues and hotels, and armoured vehicle movement is mandatory for all delegate transfers. Events that do take place, primarily at the SYL Hotel and the UN compound, require specialist close protection teams with direct Mogadishu operational experience, NISA liaison, and a medevac aircraft on confirmed standby.

item.title: Event Security in Naypyidaw
item.description:
Naypyidaw represents one of the most constrained event security environments in the world: the FCDO Myanmar advisory (2025) advises against all but essential travel, all internationally attended events are government-controlled under SAC oversight, and independent commercial event activity does not exist. International security professionals operating in this context function within the state-assigned framework, focusing on delegate welfare monitoring, contingency communications and medevac readiness.

For the full Myanmar security context, see our Naypyidaw city page. Delegates requiring personal close protection advice for Myanmar should review bodyguard hire in Naypyidaw for specialist guidance on operating in a military-governed environment.

item.title: Event Security in Niamey
item.description: Niamey’s uranium sector and ECOWAS diplomatic profile once made it a regular stop for Sahelian investment and governance conferences, but the July 2023 CNSP coup and the continued presence of JNIM and ISGS terrorism across Niger have elevated the capital to a critical-risk event destination. The FCDO Niger advisory (2025) reflects this elevated status, and professional event security combining DSP-licensed operators, soft-target venue protocols and a pre-positioned medevac contract is the minimum operational baseline.

item.title: Event Security in Port of Spain
item.description: Port of Spain’s status as the Caribbean energy sector’s primary conference hub, driven by Trinidad and Tobago’s LNG and petrochemical industries, generates consistent demand for professionally managed event security across CARICOM summits, OAS meetings and petroleum investment forums. The FCDO Trinidad and Tobago advisory (2025) highlights gang-related firearms crime and armed robbery as the principal risks, and PSIA-licensed event security is the operational baseline for any internationally attended gathering at the Hyatt Regency Trinidad or Trinidad Hilton.

item.title: Event Security in Tripoli
item.description: Tripoli’s oil and gas sector and Libya reconstruction programme generate occasional internationally attended events at the Corinthia Hotel, but the FCDO Libya advisory (2025) advises against all travel and the city’s armed faction dynamics, kidnapping risk and volatile security environment make it one of the most demanding event security contexts in the world. Every aspect of a Tripoli event requires armoured vehicle logistics, GNU and militia-liaison coordination, and a pre-positioned medevac capability.

item.title: Event Security in Baghdad
item.description: Baghdad hosts significant international events, particularly in the oil and gas sector where Iraq’s position as a major OPEC producer drives a consistent calendar of energy industry conferences. The International Conference Centre (BICC) and International Zone facilities provide the physical infrastructure. The security requirement for Baghdad events is the most complex of any regular conference destination: PMC-licensed operators, armoured delegate transport, militia-aware threat intelligence, and dedicated medical response are all standard elements of a Baghdad event security plan. FCDO advises against all but essential travel to Iraq, and event security planning must reflect that designation.

item.title: Event Security in Baku
item.description: Baku hosts significant international events in the energy sector, at the Baku Expo Centre, and the Formula 1 Azerbaijan Grand Prix, each with distinct security requirements. The energy sector conference environment requires close protection with counter-surveillance awareness for Caspian energy executives. The Grand Prix requires hospitality access control and vetted transport during circuit road closures. Azerbaijan’s MVN licensing framework under Law No. 881-IIIQ governs all deployed personnel. For the broader Baku security picture see our Baku city page and bodyguard hire in Baku.

item.title: Event Security in Bangalore
item.description: Bangalore is India’s technology capital and hosts a significant calendar of international technology summits, investor events, and corporate conferences. The FCDO terrorism assessment for India applies at low-to-moderate Bangalore risk level, and event security planning incorporates terrorism-aware access control as standard. Karnataka PSARA licensing governs all deployed personnel. The BLR-to-Whitefield transfer distance is a planning factor that requires dedicated delegate transport management. For the broader Bangalore security picture see our Bangalore city page and bodyguard hire in Bangalore.

item.title: Event Security in Beirut
item.description:
Beirut event security operates at the most complex end of the global conference security spectrum: militia zone avoidance in venue selection, hardened vehicle access control, politically-aware guest list assessment, and a medical plan that cannot rely on standard ambulance response. Ministry of Interior-licensed operators are the regulatory baseline.

For the full Beirut threat picture, see our Beirut city page. For delegates requiring personal close protection at Beirut events, bodyguard hire in Beirut covers the Ministry-licensed CPO deployment for the Lebanon engagement.

item.title: Event Security in Casablanca
item.description: Casablanca is North Africa’s primary financial hub and the preferred conference city for Africa-focused investment summits and trade events. Morocco’s terrorism threat is real and active, and DGST is one of the region’s most effective security services. Terrorism-aware venue hardening, DGST-compliant operators, and close protection calibrated to the Africa business summit delegate profile are the standard elements of Casablanca event security planning. For the broader Casablanca security picture see our Casablanca city page and bodyguard hire in Casablanca.

item.title: Event Security in Colombo
item.description:
Colombo event security operates in the context established by the 2019 Easter Sunday bombings: hotels and venues frequented by foreign nationals are the historically documented target category, and terrorism-aware venue hardening including vehicle access control and attendee screening is the standard for all international events in the city. Sri Lanka’s counter-terrorism capability has strengthened since 2019, and the city’s investment conference calendar has grown.

For the broader Colombo security context, see our Colombo city page for the current FCDO advisory and terrorism risk assessment. For delegates requiring personal close protection at Colombo events, bodyguard hire in Colombo covers police-registered close protection deployment in Sri Lanka.

item.title: Event Security in Dakar
item.description:
Dakar event security serves West Africa’s most stable major conference city, with an established international conference infrastructure, a medium risk environment, and the specific logistical consideration of the 47-kilometre Blaise Diagne Airport transfer. Ministry of Interior-registered operators are the regulatory baseline.

For the full Dakar security picture, see our Dakar city page. Delegates requiring personal close protection at Dakar events should review bodyguard hire in Dakar for the full Ministry-licensed CPO programme.

item.title: Event Security in Dar es Salaam
item.description:
Dar es Salaam event security is defined by the express kidnapping risk profile that applies to high-value international delegates, the carjacking-aware airport transfer protocol, and the limited local medical infrastructure. PSRA-licensed operators under the Private Security Act 2008 are the compliance foundation.

For the broader Dar es Salaam security context, see our Dar es Salaam city page. Delegates requiring personal close protection at Dar es Salaam events should review bodyguard hire in Dar es Salaam for the full PSRA-licensed CPO programme.

item.title: Event Security in Delhi
item.description: Delhi is India’s national capital and host to significant international events including the 2023 G20 Presidency Summit at the Bharat Mandapam International Exhibition and Convention Centre. The terrorism threat assessed by FCDO as very likely across India, combined with Delhi’s high-profile target status, means event security planning incorporates terrorism-aware venue hardening as standard. India’s PSARA 2005 framework licenses operators and individual personnel at state level. For the broader Delhi security picture see our Delhi city page and bodyguard hire in Delhi.

item.title: Event Security in Dhaka
item.description:
Dhaka event security is shaped by the 2016 Holey Artisan attack’s legacy of upgraded security infrastructure in Gulshan, the persistent hartal disruption risk that requires logistics contingency in a way that few other major capitals demand, and the traffic conditions that make emergency medical response a specific planning requirement at every event.

For the broader Dhaka security context, see our Dhaka city page for the current FCDO terrorism advisory and hartal risk assessment. For delegates requiring personal close protection at Dhaka events, bodyguard hire in Dhaka covers Ministry of Home Affairs-licensed close protection deployment in Bangladesh.

item.title: Event Security in Geneva
item.description: Geneva is Europe’s multilateral conference hub, hosting the UN European headquarters, WTO, WHO, ICRC, and a significant private banking and HNWI event calendar. FCDO notes the active intelligence environment around Geneva’s multilateral processes. Swiss cantonal licensing under Geneva’s LPSP governs all deployed security personnel. HNWI event close protection is calibrated to the discreet professional standard appropriate to Switzerland’s financial environment. For the broader Geneva security picture see our Geneva city page and bodyguard hire in Geneva.

item.title: Event Security in Guadalajara
item.description:
Guadalajara event security manages a high-risk Mexican state environment with specific CJNG cartel awareness, hardened venue access, and a federal-and-state licensing compliance requirement. The technology and investment event sector that defines Guadalajara’s international corporate calendar requires a security programme calibrated to the specific risk profile of Mexico’s second city.

For the full Guadalajara security picture and FCDO Mexico advisory, see our Guadalajara city page. Delegates requiring personal close protection at Guadalajara events should review bodyguard hire in Guadalajara for the full licensed CPO programme.

item.title: Event Security in Harare
item.description:
Harare event security addresses Zimbabwe’s specific operational challenges: economic instability logistics (currency, fuel, power), political sensitivity around the government quarter, and limited local medical infrastructure requiring a Johannesburg evacuation plan as the primary protocol for complex cases. Zimbabwe Republic Police-licensed operators are the regulatory foundation.

For the full Harare security picture and FCDO Zimbabwe advisory, see our Harare city page. Delegates requiring personal close protection at Harare events should review bodyguard hire in Harare for the ZRP-licensed CPO programme.

item.title: Event Security in Ho Chi Minh City
item.description:
Ho Chi Minh City is Vietnam’s commercial capital and Southeast Asia’s most active FDI conference market outside Singapore. Event security addresses the city’s specific profile: motorbike snatching awareness at hotel venue pedestrian approaches, digital surveillance environment briefings for commercially sensitive events, and Ministry of Public Security-licensed operators managing the delegate experience in a market where international operator independence is constrained.

For the broader HCMC security context, see our Ho Chi Minh City page for the Ministry of Public Security licensing framework and district-level risk assessment. For delegates requiring personal close protection at HCMC events, bodyguard hire in Ho Chi Minh City covers licensed close protection deployment in Vietnam.

item.title: Event Security in Kampala
item.description:
Kampala event security operates in a high-risk East African capital environment where ADF terrorism, boda boda snatching, and limited medical capacity all require specific planning provisions beyond standard African capital event security. UPF-licensed operators, terrorism-aware venue hardening, and documented medical evacuation plans are the foundations of the Kampala event security programme.

For the broader Kampala security context, see our Kampala city page for the current FCDO terrorism advisory and district-level risk assessment. For delegates requiring personal close protection at Kampala events, bodyguard hire in Kampala covers UPF-licensed close protection deployment across Uganda.

item.title: Event Security in Kigali
item.description:
Kigali event security serves East Africa’s safest conference capital, with a low-crime operating environment, well-structured PSRB regulatory framework, and a specific advisory requirement for political expression restrictions relevant to civil society or human rights events. The event security specification is substantially lower than peer East African capitals.

For the full Kigali security context and Rwanda travel advisory, see our Kigali city page. Delegates requiring personal close protection at Kigali events should review bodyguard hire in Kigali for the PSRB-licensed CPO programme.

item.title: Event Security in Kyiv
item.description:
Kyiv event security exists for gatherings that must take place in Ukraine: diplomatic summits, reconstruction conferences, and donor coordination events that cannot be relocated outside the conflict zone. The FCDO advises against all travel to Ukraine. Every Kyiv event begins with venue shelter capacity assessment and a documented air raid suspension protocol before any other logistics are planned.

For the broader Kyiv wartime security context, see our Kyiv city page for the strike pattern overview and the FCDO against-all-travel advisory. For delegates requiring personal close protection throughout the Kyiv event programme, close protection officers in Kyiv covers wartime CPO deployment and overland entry coordination.

item.title: Event Security in Lima
item.description:
Lima event security is defined by the express kidnapping risk profile that applies to high-value international delegates, particularly those in the mining and finance sectors, and by the SUCAMEC licensing framework for all event security personnel. Lima’s well-developed private medical infrastructure is a relative advantage in the regional event security picture.

For the full Lima security context and FCDO Peru advisory, see our Lima city page. Delegates requiring personal close protection at Lima events should review bodyguard hire in Lima for the SUCAMEC-registered CPO programme.

item.title: Event Security in Luanda
item.description:
Luanda event security serves Angola’s high-cost, high-crime capital, with specific requirements for petty crime prevention, Ministry of Interior-licensed delegate transport, and a Johannesburg-based medical evacuation plan. The oil sector event calendar drives most of the international conference demand for Luanda event security services.

For the full Luanda security picture, see our Luanda city page. Delegates requiring personal close protection at Luanda events should review bodyguard hire in Luanda for the Ministry-licensed CPO programme.

item.title: Event Security in Lusaka
item.description:
Lusaka event security serves a medium-risk southern African copper belt capital, with Zambia Police Service-licensed operators, specific evening movement management outside the hotel zone, and a Johannesburg-based medical evacuation plan. The mining and copper sector event calendar defines most of the international conference demand for Lusaka.

For the full Lusaka security context, see our Lusaka city page. Delegates requiring personal close protection at Lusaka events should review bodyguard hire in Lusaka for the Zambia Police-licensed CPO programme.

item.title: Event Security in Maputo
item.description:
Maputo event security addresses Mozambique’s high-risk capital environment with CSPN-licensed operators, specific street crime prevention protocol for the Baixa district, and a Johannesburg-based medical evacuation plan. The LNG and energy sector conference calendar that defines Maputo’s growing international event profile also requires a Cabo Delgado awareness component for delegates with northern Mozambique operational responsibilities.

For the full Maputo security context, see our Maputo city page. Delegates requiring personal close protection at Maputo events should review bodyguard hire in Maputo for the CSPN-licensed CPO programme.

item.title: Event Security in Mombasa
item.description:
Mombasa event security operates in one of the higher-risk environments on the East African coast, where Al-Shabaab’s documented presence in the region and the Kenya coast’s elevated terrorism advisory require event security provisions substantially above what comparable East African cities demand. PSIRA-licensed operators, vehicle access control, and strict night movement management are the foundations of the Mombasa event security programme.

For the broader Mombasa security context, see our Mombasa city page for the current FCDO terrorism advisory and port sector risk assessment. For delegates requiring personal close protection at Mombasa events, bodyguard hire in Mombasa covers PSIRA-licensed close protection deployment on the Kenya coast.

item.title: Event Security in Montreal
item.description: Montreal hosts significant international events including the Formula 1 Canadian Grand Prix, the International Jazz Festival, and major medical and pharmaceutical congresses at the Palais des congres. Quebec’s Bureau de la securite privee licensing framework governs all deployed security personnel. The Grand Prix circuit island format creates specific access control and transport management challenges. CSIS’s foreign state interference assessments are relevant for aerospace and technology conferences. For the broader Montreal security picture see our Montreal city page and bodyguard hire in Montreal.

item.title: Event Security in Muscat
item.description:
Muscat’s combination of Oman’s diplomatic neutrality, low overall risk profile, and growing conference infrastructure makes it a preferred venue for sensitive GCC and regional events alongside standard investment and energy sector forums. Royal Oman Police-licensed event security, summer heat planning, and cultural compliance management are the specific elements of the Muscat event security programme.

For the broader Muscat security context, see our Muscat city page for the ROP licensing framework and regional security assessment. For delegates requiring personal close protection at Muscat events, bodyguard hire in Muscat covers ROP-licensed close protection deployment across Oman.

item.title: Event Security in Naples
item.description:
Naples is Italy’s southern capital and one of Europe’s most atmospheric settings for corporate and HNWI events, with the Chiaia waterfront, Posillipo hilltop, and the Amalfi Coast providing venues that attract significant international HNWI demand. GPG-licensed event security addresses the city’s specific profile: the Camorra contractor due diligence requirement, scooter-borne theft awareness for pedestrian delegate movement, and the coastal and island access management that Campania events uniquely require.

For the broader Naples security context, see our Naples city page for the full Camorra operating environment and district-level risk assessment. For delegates requiring personal close protection throughout the Naples event programme, our close protection officers in Naples covers GPG-licensed CPO deployment across Campania.

item.title: Event Security in Rome
item.description: Rome is Italy’s seat of government, home to the Vatican, and a significant international conference city with major venues including La Nuvola at EUR, which hosted the G20 Rome Summit in 2021. Italy’s terrorism threat is rated high by FCDO, and terrorism-aware venue hardening is standard for government-profile events. Italian GPG licensing under the TULPS framework governs all deployed close protection personnel. For the broader Rome security picture see our Rome city page and bodyguard hire in Rome.

item.title: Event Security in Shanghai
item.description: Shanghai is China’s global business capital and hosts major international trade events, including the China International Import Expo (CIIE) at the world-scale National Exhibition and Convention Centre. China’s national security law framework creates specific risk considerations that require pre-event delegate briefing. PSB oversight means foreign security companies must operate through Chinese-licensed partner operators. For the broader Shanghai security picture see our Shanghai city page and executive protection in Shanghai.

item.title: Event Security in Tashkent
item.description:
Tashkent is emerging as Central Asia’s primary conference and investment forum destination as Uzbekistan’s post-2016 economic reform programme draws increasing international corporate and government attention.
